name: Embalse de Aguascebas - circular through the P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
name_original: Embalse de Aguascebas - circular por el P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
region_intro: Hiking around Villacarrillo offers access to the diverse landscapes of Jaén province, characterized by extensive olive groves and cereal fields. The region borders the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park, Spain's largest protected area, featuring vast pine forests, rocky crests, and deep gorges. This natural park is recognized as a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Villacarrillo The most popular hiking route is Río Borosa Trail, a 11.6 miles (18.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 57 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses gorges and leads past impressive waterfalls. Another top favourite among local hikers is Borosa River Route – Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park, a moderate 6.8 miles (11.0 km) path. This trail offers views through the natural park's varied terrain. Local hikers also love the La Osera Trail, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail leading through the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park, often completed in about 2 hours 5 minutes. Hiking around Villacarrillo is defined by olive groves, vast pine forests, and rocky crests, with trails often featuring gorges and waterfalls. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more demanding routes. The routes in Villacarrillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Villacarrillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Villacarrillo?
A: There are over 25 hiking trails around Villacarrillo, offering a variety of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking near Villacarrillo?
A: Hiking around Villacarrillo offers diverse landscapes, from extensive olive groves and cereal fields to the vast pine forests and rocky crests of the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park. You'll often encounter deep gorges and impressive waterfalls within the park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Villacarrillo?
A: The Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park, accessible from Villacarrillo, is beautiful year-round. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking, with lush greenery in spring and vibrant colors in autumn. Summers can be hot, while winters may bring cooler temperatures and occasional snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any hikes with waterfalls near Villacarrillo?
A: Yes, several trails feature impressive waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980001275/rio-borosa-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Borosa Trail&lt;/a&gt; is renowned for traversing gorges and leading past spectacular waterfalls like Salto de los Órganos. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988228155/la-osera-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Osera Trail&lt;/a&gt; also offers stunning waterfall views.

Q: What interesting natural attractions or viewpoints can I find along the trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can find viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from the Fuente de la Parra shelter&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Cabeza de la Viña Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. There are also beautiful lakes and reservoirs such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3168204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Aceite&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranco de Beas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options around Villacarrillo?
A: Yes, the area offers diverse options for various fitness levels. While some routes are challenging, there are also moderate and easy paths suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988228155/la-osera-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Osera Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.6-mile route that can be completed in about 2 hours, making it a good option for active families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails near Villacarrillo?
A: Many trails within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park are generally dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety. Check specific park regulations or trail signs for any restrictions before heading out.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Villacarrillo?
A: Yes, there are circular hiking routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988224360/embalse-de-aguascebas-circular-through-the-p-n-de-las-sierras-de-cazorla-segura-y-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Aguascebas - circular through the P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 8.6 km loop.

Q: Is public transport available to access the hiking areas from Villacarrillo?
A: While Villacarrillo itself has bus connections, direct public transport to the trailheads within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park can be limited. It's often more convenient to use a private vehicle to reach the starting points of many popular hikes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Villacarrillo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning natural beauty, particularly the dramatic gorges and waterfalls, and the sense of immersion in Spain's largest protected natural area.

Q: Are there any permits required for hiking in the Sierras de Cazorla Natural Park?
A: Generally, permits are not required for standard hiking on marked trails within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park. However, certain activities or access to specific restricted areas might require prior authorization. It's advisable to check with the park's official information points for the most current regulations, especially for larger groups or specific activities.

Q: Where can I find parking at trailheads near Villacarrillo?
A: Many popular trailheads, especially those leading into the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park, typically have designated parking areas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980001275/rio-borosa-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Borosa Trail&lt;/a&gt; often starts from an area with parking facilities near a power station. It's recommended to arrive early during peak seasons to secure a spot.
